jokes.text: d'oh!
jokes.punchline: The iconic 'D'oh!' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, culturally significant

jokes.text: Won't you marry me, bill? I got the wedding gown bill because weddings are Nice
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'bill' as both a name and an invoice
jokes.explanation: Basic wordplay pun, not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: The kbbl prize posse! Damn dirty ape! If our wampum wagon spots your kbbl party penguin, you'll win $40!
jokes.setup: Radio contest with elaborate marketing gimmicks
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top promotional language for a measly $40 prize
jokes.explanation: Good satire of radio promotion excess, but not exceptional

jokes.text: If we're late for school, we'll miss our free federal breakfast! Big deal. It's just saltines and fig paste. Ew! Saltines!
jokes.setup: Concern about missing free school breakfast
jokes.punchline: The breakfast is terrible, but Milhouse is disgusted by the wrong part
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with character-specific reactions

jokes.text: Hey, Cora. I heard science is working on a donut that actually burns off calories. How's that going?
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate hope for guilt-free indulgence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - wanting science to enable his vices

jokes.text: Just refill this with jelly, Will you? Thanks. You're an angel. What?
jokes.setup: Homer asking for donut refill and calling worker an angel
jokes.punchline: Cora's confused 'What?' reaction to the over-the-top compliment
jokes.explanation: Nice reaction beat showing Homer's excessive enthusiasm

jokes.text: With rain baggy! Oh, man. That would really keep your head dry.
jokes.punchline: Being most impressed by the rain protection
jokes.explanation: Childish priorities - focusing on mundane practical benefit

jokes.text: Have you ever been in a police car? Not in the front.
jokes.setup: Innocent question about police car experience
jokes.punchline: Milhouse has been arrested before
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - dark implication delivered perfectly

jokes.text: You there! Put your hands up! Me? Okay. Now, drop your pants. Yeah, but... But my hands are up. Hula out of them.
jokes.punchline: Absurd command to 'hula out of' pants while hands are up
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with physical comedy potential

jokes.text: It's me, Clancy. No! Get away!
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum identifying himself to his own car
jokes.punchline: Bart's panicked rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat, functional but not memorable

jokes.text: Soup! Soup!
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre exclamation during crisis
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer non-sequitur, perfectly in character

jokes.text: You have the right to remain, um... Uh... Silent? That doesn't sound right.
jokes.punchline: He doesn't know the most basic police procedure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - great character integration

jokes.text: I love our court days. It's about the only thing we do as a family anymore.
jokes.punchline: Court appearances are their main family activity
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect Marge resignation, excellent structure

jokes.text: He's basically a good kid. He's just weak. Morally and in the upper body.
jokes.setup: Homer defending Bart to the judge
jokes.punchline: Adding physical weakness as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from moral to physical weakness, very Homer

jokes.text: Please let me slip through the cracks.
jokes.punchline: Openly asking to escape justice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart honesty about wanting to avoid consequences

jokes.text: Well, you look like a good student, what with those glasses, and I suppose boys Will be boys. Case dismissed.
jokes.punchline: Completely superficial judgment based on appearance
jokes.explanation: Good satire of judicial bias, shows Snyder's incompetence

jokes.text: Why, hello, Bart. Say, are those new shoes? Yes, they are, Roy.
jokes.punchline: Casual conversation during formal proceedings, and Bart calls him Roy
jokes.explanation: Shows inappropriate familiarity between judge and repeat offender

jokes.text: I'll bring the car around. And I suppose boys Will be... Oops. My vacation just started.
jokes.setup: Judge about to give his standard lenient ruling
jokes.punchline: Abruptly abandoning Bart when vacation starts
jokes.explanation: Good timing on the reversal, shows Bart's luck running out

jokes.text: Don't spit on my cupcake and tell me it's frosting.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming the theft was an accident
jokes.punchline: Judge's colorful way of saying 'don't lie to me'
jokes.explanation: Creative vulgar expression, establishes Judge Harm's character

jokes.text: You abandoned your son to win $40? And a blue oyster cult medallion. Cool.
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm for the worthless prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer missing the point, good misdirection

jokes.text: Your honor, if I may sing a little bit of don't fear the reaper, I think you'll agree that...
jokes.setup: Homer trying to defend himself in court
jokes.punchline: His defense strategy is singing Blue Oyster Cult
jokes.explanation: Absurd Homer logic - thinks music will solve legal problems

jokes.text: Perhaps some sort of court-appointed babysitter or 'au pair.'
jokes.punchline: Homer suggesting he needs a babysitter
jokes.explanation: Homer completely missing that he's the problem

jokes.text: Sorry, bub. That crow won't caw.
jokes.punchline: Judge's creative way of saying 'that won't work'
jokes.explanation: Another colorful Judge Harm expression, less memorable than cupcake line

jokes.text: Sir, you are not a size four. I used to be.
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional claim about his past size
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline with Homer's self-delusion

jokes.text: That's why Bill Clinton is our new mailman.
jokes.punchline: Absurd example of presidential punishment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the creative sentencing concept

jokes.text: Dang magazines.
jokes.punchline: Grumbling about his new job
jokes.explanation: Good followup to the Clinton joke, shows him actually doing the job

jokes.text: Make sure your father takes his mood medication. I'll medicate you... honey.
jokes.punchline: Homer's threatening response, caught and corrected
jokes.explanation: Homer's aggressive impulse checked by social propriety, revealing his volatility

jokes.text: Race you to the kitchen, my little tether ball. You're on, rope-a-dope!
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart making the best of their situation
jokes.punchline: Both using rope-related nicknames
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing father-son bonding through adversity

jokes.text: Boring! Mr. Simpson, I'm trying to teach. Come on. These kids are never gonna use that stuff.
jokes.punchline: Homer's anti-education attitude
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer anti-intellectualism, perfectly in character

jokes.text: What's wrong with him now, Bart? Night terrors, ma'am. Cobras!
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific cobra-related night terror
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline, specific fear is funnier than generic nightmare

jokes.text: Hey, hey! Run, Bart! Pump your thighs! Pump them!
jokes.punchline: Homer's overly specific coaching
jokes.explanation: Homer's awkward sports dad behavior enhanced by tether situation

jokes.text: Safe! Wow. My first home run. That's my boy.
jokes.setup: Bart succeeding at baseball for once
jokes.punchline: Getting a 'home run' on what was clearly a close play at first
jokes.explanation: Bart's confusion about baseball terminology, sweet father-son moment

jokes.text: Come on, hug me. [Awkward pause]
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to celebrate Bart's home run
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of trying to hug while tethered
jokes.explanation: Visual gag potential with tether complicating normal father-son moment

jokes.text: Cobras! Cobras!
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific phobia recurring
jokes.explanation: Good callback to establish Homer's recurring phobia

jokes.text: And there's no one here to squeal on me for shooting mice.
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to animal cruelty at work
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Homer's workplace misconduct

jokes.text: Oh, son, you know that's none of your business.
jokes.punchline: Homer treating electricity as classified information
jokes.explanation: Good Homer deflection, treating basic question as inappropriate

jokes.text: Say, is that our house? Uh, I don't think our house has a steeple. Yeah. I forget things sometimes.
jokes.punchline: He's looking at a church and casually admits memory problems
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from confusion to admission, very Homer

jokes.text: Hey, hey, no kids in the bar! Since when? The heat's been on since them bush girls were in here.
jokes.punchline: Reference to underage Bush daughters causing problems
jokes.explanation: Topical humor about presidential daughters, good setup-punchline

jokes.text: Hey, knock it off! These pants cost $600. Really? Yeah. They're Italian. All right, hand them over. Moe, what the... Yeah, I Rob now.
jokes.punchline: Moe immediately deciding to rob him
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character moment - Moe's casual criminality

jokes.text: If you can't cope, you'll wear the rope.
jokes.setup: Judge Harm on TV explaining her philosophy
jokes.punchline: Catchy rhyme about tether punishment
jokes.explanation: Memorable rhyme that encapsulates her judicial philosophy

jokes.text: Well, you can tell she's never had kids. Look how high and firm her breasts are.
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific physical observation
jokes.explanation: Unexpected line from Marge, good observational humor

jokes.text: Use the bottle. No! I don't want you going in a bottle. That's what hobos do.
jokes.setup: Bart needs bathroom, Homer suggests bottle
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific objection about hobos
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Homer's laziness vs Marge's standards

jokes.text: Fine! I don't know why we even have a bottle! Somebody tell me!
jokes.setup: Homer being forced to take Bart to bathroom
jokes.punchline: Confused about the bottle's purpose
jokes.explanation: Homer's confusion about basic household items

jokes.text: Son, it's a little chilly. Maybe you should wrap a blanket around your head.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge trying to be intimate with Bart present
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate suggestion for dealing with the situation
jokes.explanation: Awkward parenting moment, Homer's bizarre solution

jokes.text: Why not? Kids are very visual these days!
jokes.setup: Marge objecting to intimacy in front of Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely wrong reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic fail - misunderstanding modern kids entirely

jokes.text: The lady said no. Hey, shut up. You shut up.
jokes.punchline: Immediate devolution into childish fighting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's immaturity

jokes.text: I'm sleeping in the bathtub. Why, you little...
jokes.punchline: Marge's dramatic exit and Homer starting to strangle
jokes.explanation: Standard Simpsons family dysfunction escalation

jokes.text: I can't take it anymore!
jokes.setup: Physical fight between tethered Homer and Bart
jokes.punchline: Marge's breaking point after enduring the chaos
jokes.explanation: Marge's reasonable reaction to unreasonable situation

jokes.text: Mom, you cut the tether. We're free! Your mother set us free!
jokes.punchline: Biblical liberation language for mundane situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation using freedom/slavery metaphor

jokes.text: That's right. It's me, judge harm, through the magic of fiber optics.
jokes.punchline: Judge monitoring them remotely with casual tech explanation
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - high-tech surveillance with casual delivery

jokes.text: Hey, hey, if I want a cock and bull story, I'll read Hemingway.
jokes.punchline: Literary reference dismissing his excuse
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: You see, I was pressuring my wife to make love in front of our son, you're gonna laugh when you hear this, when suddenly...
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain to judge
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing thinking it's funny
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - Homer completely misjudging the situation

jokes.text: It's a miracle poor Bartholomew isn't robbing banks and chasing sweet lady h.
jokes.punchline: Extreme criminal activities including drug reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurd criminal outcomes

jokes.text: I'm a latchkey kid. You are not!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dramatic claim vs Marge's defensive response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lisa's drama vs Marge's sensitivity

jokes.text: I'm a latchkey kid. You are not!
jokes.punchline: Bart claiming neglect, Marge's indignant correction
jokes.explanation: Bart trying to game the system, Marge defending her parenting

jokes.text: And frankly, judge, I think you're a bully!
jokes.setup: Marge finally standing up to judge
jokes.punchline: Calling a judge a bully to her face
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - maternal protectiveness overcoming politeness

jokes.text: Your honor, I'd like to be tried separately.
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately abandoning her
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Homer's cowardice perfectly timed

jokes.text: She's such a butthole.
jokes.setup: Judge commenting on Marge after she leaves
jokes.punchline: Professional judge using childish insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing judge's unprofessionalism

jokes.text: 'Cause I felt like it. You're not even listening. I know you are, but what am I?
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to make Bart take responsibility
jokes.punchline: Bart's complete dismissal and childish comeback
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Bart's immaturity and avoidance

jokes.text: Yarr, ye scurvy dogs! Ow! Ow! Worst parents ever! Ha-ha! Hey! No extension cords!
jokes.punchline: Various townspeople getting involved, Homer objecting to extension cords
jokes.explanation: Good ensemble moment with multiple character voices

jokes.text: It could be 'negligent' or 'unfit' or 'drugged up.'
jokes.punchline: Progressively worse descriptors
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer making things worse

jokes.text: It's time to stand up to that firm-breasted judge.
jokes.punchline: Repeating Marge's earlier physical observation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Marge's earlier observation, now adopted by Homer

jokes.text: What the... Hey, Marge, surf's up!
jokes.setup: Ned discovering them breaking out of stocks
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual greeting while using power tools
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat with Homer's inappropriate casualness

jokes.text: Gee, I always like to help you, homer, but I don't want to be an accessory to some sort of shady doings.
jokes.setup: Homer using Ned's tools to break court order
jokes.punchline: Ned's overly polite way of backing out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character work - helpful but law-abiding

jokes.text: If that costume shop knew we were using these burglar outfits for real, they'd be furious.
jokes.setup: Wearing burglar costumes for actual crime
jokes.punchline: Marge worried about costume shop's reaction, not the crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - worried about propriety even while committing crimes

jokes.text: Let's start skulking.
jokes.punchline: Using 'skulking' as an action verb
jokes.explanation: Good word choice, treating skulking like a planned activity

jokes.text: Oh. It's only the milkman. Hey, maybe I should be a milkman!
jokes.punchline: Homer getting distracted by career thoughts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ADD moment - getting distracted during crime

jokes.text: She lives in a houseboat? Wow! She is so cool!
jokes.punchline: Homer admiring his enemy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's inability to stay focused on revenge

jokes.text: We hate her, homer. I know. I know. Fight the power.
jokes.setup: Marge reminding Homer they're supposed to hate judge
jokes.punchline: Homer's halfhearted revolutionary slogan
jokes.explanation: Homer's weak commitment to their mission despite personal stakes

jokes.text: Ain't that sweet, chief? It sure is, Lou. Those two longshoremen found love.
jokes.setup: Police observing Homer and Marge in burglar outfits
jokes.punchline: Misidentifying them as romantic dock workers
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding gag with police incompetence

jokes.text: I hate to call a judge dirty names, but there's only one way to describe a nasty super witch like her.
jokes.punchline: Building up to insult while already insulting her
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic fail - insulting while claiming reluctance to insult

jokes.text: It's just a friendly seal.
jokes.punchline: Homer's naive assessment of wild animal
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misreading dangerous situation

jokes.text: Oh, lord, guide this cinder block. Homer, no!
jokes.setup: Homer about to throw cinder block at houseboat
jokes.punchline: Praying for divine guidance in property destruction
jokes.explanation: Homer seeking religious sanction for vandalism

jokes.text: Hey, how you doing?
jokes.setup: Homer emerging from judge's destroyed houseboat
jokes.punchline: Casual greeting after causing major property damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery after destruction, very Homer

jokes.text: So... it cost you nothing. Shut up!
jokes.setup: Judge upset about grandmother's quilt being destroyed
jokes.punchline: Homer's terrible logic about inherited items
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - dismissing sentimental value with economics

jokes.text: and I'm gonna lock you up till frogs do fractions.
jokes.punchline: Colorful way of saying 'forever'
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist threat, fits Judge Harm's colorful speech pattern

jokes.text: You remind me of me when I was a little boy.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of gender change
jokes.explanation: Excellent throwaway line with major implications, perfect economy

jokes.text: Um... did she say she used to be a dude?
jokes.setup: Judge's previous comment about being a boy
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed processing of the information
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Homer's typical slow processing

jokes.text: Well, I'm back from vacation. But I was just about to bang my gavel, making the sentence official. Sorry, I've already put my clown down. But I was just going to... The clown is down.
jokes.setup: Judge Snyder returning to save the day
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous formality about putting down the clown
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist bureaucracy - the clown symbolism is perfect

jokes.text: Judge snyder, motion to declare a writ of boys Will be boys. Motion granted. Case dismissed!
jokes.punchline: Making 'boys will be boys' an official legal motion
jokes.explanation: Great callback turning casual phrase into legal terminology

jokes.text: Motion granted. Case dismissed!
jokes.setup: Motion for 'boys will be boys' defense
jokes.punchline: Return to lenient judge's standard ruling
jokes.explanation: Return to status quo with familiar judge's lenient pattern

jokes.text: That was close. Please drive off me.
jokes.punchline: Homer inadvertently crushing Bart during hug
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy ending with Homer's characteristic clumsiness

jokes.text: What's that noise? Uh... just the radio, dear.
jokes.punchline: Homer lying while someone is obviously trapped in trunk
jokes.explanation: Good setup for visual gag with Homer's unconvincing lie

jokes.text: cobras!
jokes.setup: Don't Fear the Reaper song ending
jokes.punchline: Homer's nightmare interrupting peaceful moment
jokes.explanation: Final callback to running gag, ending episode with familiar character beat
